Transcrição Completa: A Praga dos Fat Cat Closers

[00:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "2 The Plague of Fat Cat Closers"

NÚMEROS: None

VISUAL: Título em destaque com tom agressivo, possivelmente cores mais intensas

[00:02] Okay, so there's two types of sales people within an organization. There's this absolute dog shit type of salesperson referred to as a fat cat closer. And then there's what we call real salespeople.

[00:15] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "Two Types of Salespeople"

NÚMEROS: None

VISUAL: Comparação visual dividida - lado esquerdo "Fat Cat Closers" vs lado direito "Real Salespeople"

[00:18] To be very direct, I'm going to label this lazy type of salesperson as a fat cat closer because we have to be on the same page from from a vernacular perspective. So we can label the type of behavior we do not want to see within an organization.

[00:32] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "The Problem: VSSL Funnel Statistics"

NÚMEROS: 2,000 people hit page, 5% pressed play = 100 people

VISUAL: Funil visual mostrando drastica redução de números, gráfico de conversão descendente

[00:40] A lot of sales people over the last three to four years have gotten really used to only taking calls after somebody has watched some type of webinar or video or VSSL or whatever and then they fill out an application slash they schedule a call.

[01:20] I saw that with this type of funnel, we're going to refer to it as a VSSL funnel, a webinar funnel, a mini webinar funnel, whatever you want to call it. I looked up one particular client, 2,000 people hit the page. Out of that 2,000 people, 5% pressed play.

[01:45] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "The Bottleneck Problem"

NÚMEROS: 2,000 in → 100 press play → 5-10 schedule calls → 2-3 show up

VISUAL: Funil estreito mostrando gargalo extremo, fluxo visual com perdas massivas

[01:50] That is 100 people that once again didn't watch the video, they just pressed play. That does not mean they watched the video. That means they pressed play. Okay, this is what we call a bottleneck.

[02:30] In an ideal scenario, it would look like this. 2,000 people come in the funnel, 2,000 people press play, 2,000 people schedule a call or fill out an application, 2,000 people show up to the call. You and I both know that's shit and that's never how it works in real marketing and advertising.

[03:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "Reality vs Ideal Funnel Performance"

NÚMEROS: Ideal: 2,000 → 2,000 → 2,000 | Reality: 2,000 → 100 → 5 → 2-3

VISUAL: Comparação lado a lado, ideal vs realidade com números chocantes

[03:15] From here, you and I both know 100 people won't schedule a call. How many of them are actually going to watch the video? How many of them are then actually going to go to the next step and let's say schedule a call? Maybe it's five, maybe it's 10.

[04:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "Car Dealership Analogy"

NÚMEROS: 2,000 visitors → 100 talk to someone → 5 sit with salesperson

VISUAL: Ícones de concessionária, carros, pessoas, mostrando absurdo da situação

[04:30] So essentially, imagine you ran a store where you, let's say you had a car dealership. 2,000 people show up per day to your car dealership. Only a hundred of them end up even talking to somebody at your dealership. And out of those, only five people end up sitting down with a salesperson.

[05:30] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "Fat Cat Closers vs Real Salespeople"

NÚMEROS: None

VISUAL: Contraste visual forte - fat cats preguiçosos vs vendedores ativos e energéticos

[05:45] A real salesperson would be so enthused to have the opportunity to talk to more than five people in a day. A fat cat closer only wants to talk to people. They want to constrain the business. They want to hold back the amount of money the business can make because they're selfish.

[06:30] Real sales people, dude, they'll talk to as many people that are qualified as they can. They'll talk to many people. They want the additional opportunities. They'll outbound dial to leads that have expressed interest and fill out forms. They'll follow up.

[07:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "Fat Cat Closer = Cashier"

NÚMEROS: None

VISUAL: Comparação visual brutal - ícone de caixa registradora = fat cat closer

[07:15] A fat cat closer is literally no better than a cashier. That's right. Every fat cat closer listening to this. Go to your LinkedIn, update your job title to fucking cashier because that's all you are if you're a fat cat closer.

[08:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "Cost Analysis: VSSL vs Hydra Strategy"

NÚMEROS: VSSL: $100 per call ($200 actual), Hydra: $10-15 per lead

VISUAL: Comparação de custos, gráficos mostrando eficiência 5-10x maior

[08:30] If this costs, let's say an average of $100 cost per call, and we've seen less and we've seen more. Average cost per call in a business might be anywhere from $50 to $150. So this is actually 2x the cost and it ends up being $200 per connected call.

[09:30] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "The Hydra Strategy"

NÚMEROS: 2,000 visitors → 100-200 form fills → 5-10x efficiency

VISUAL: Formulário de lead, múltiplas cabeças de hidra representando múltiplas oportunidades

[09:45] There's this current ad strategy. It works unbelievably well for businesses with real salespeople and not fat cat closers. It's called the Hydra. And the hydra ad strategy is very simple. We put a lead form in front of the VSSL funnels.

[11:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "Lead Form Efficiency"

NÚMEROS: $3-4 per lead (general), $10-20 (high-ticket), $15 average

VISUAL: Formulários, dados de contato, perguntas qualificadoras

[11:30] I get leads about $3 to $4 a piece. We've seen them come in for clients that are raising a hundred k on average from accredited investors for anywhere from like 10 to 20. So, let's use the example. It averages out to about $15 a pop.

[12:30] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "Connection Rate Comparison"

NÚMEROS: Hydra: 2/5 connect (40%), VSSL: 50% show rate

VISUAL: Gráficos de conexão, telefones, indicadores de efetividade

[13:00] The connection rate on this type of funnel, you're typically going to see two out of five people connect, meaning they'll pick up the phone and they'll have a full-blown sales conversation with you.

[14:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "Real Salespeople Make More Money"

NÚMEROS: 5-10x more opportunities = more revenue

VISUAL: Gráficos de receita, vendedores reais vs fat cats, dados de performance

[14:30] When we run these strategies inside of teams that have real salespeople, the real salespeople make far more money in comparison to the fat cat closers any day of the week.

[15:30] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "Consumer Behavior Has Changed"

NÚMEROS: 20-50% → 5% video engagement (3-4 years decline)

VISUAL: Gráfico temporal mostrando declínio de engajamento em vídeos

[16:00] I've ran call funnels for the last 3 to four years. We've done dozens of businesses that make millions of dollars a month specifically through call funnels cuz they worked great. But that was when 20 to 50% of people would hit the page and press play. That numbers trended down, not up.

[17:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "Modern Consumer Preferences"

NÚMEROS: None

VISUAL: Ícones de formulários, telefone, texto, representando preferências atuais

[17:15] They're filling out lead forms directly expressing interest and they want to talk to somebody on the phone. They want to talk to somebody over text. They want to get to know a little bit about the business before they even spend 10 simple minutes watching a video.

[18:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "This Training Builds Real Salespeople"

NÚMEROS: None

VISUAL: Construção/treinamento de vendedores reais, eliminação de fat cats

[18:15] This entire sales training program that you're about to go through is built to build real salespeople. The things I'm going to talk about in this are for real sales people.

[18:45] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "Update Your LinkedIn: You're a Cashier"

NÚMEROS: None

VISUAL: Logo do LinkedIn, título "Cashier" destacado mockingly

[19:00] This absolute hunk of trash, this cashier. Make sure you go update your LinkedIn profiles. Like I said, you're a cashier. You're not a salesperson if this is you.

[19:30] We need real sales people. And I'm excited to help you get there. And more importantly, I'm excited to help make your business a fuckload of more money by biasing towards having real sales people in the organization.

🔥 ANÁLISE FURION POWER: Jeremy destrói IMPIEDOSAMENTE a mentalidade fraca dos "fat cat closers"! Esta é uma DECLARAÇÃO DE GUERRA contra a mediocridade em vendas. Ele expõe com DADOS BRUTAIS como esses vendedores preguiçosos estão ESTRANGULANDO os negócios. A estratégia Hydra é pura GENIALIDADE - transformar 2.000 oportunidades perdidas em centenas de leads qualificados. Jeremy não apenas identifica o problema, ele ANIQUILA a desculpa e oferece a SOLUÇÃO DOMINANTE. Um verdadeiro MESTRE das vendas eliminando os fracos do campo de batalha!
